title: crotalaria ephemera
intro: Crotalaria ephemera, also known as the ephemeral rattlebox, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its annual or biennial growth habit and distinctive rattle-like seeds. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Crotalaria ephemera are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be administered orally for various health purposes.
